Description: Start of the Gulf War causing oil supply concerns.
Sharp decline in Asian demand for oil.
US-led invasion of Iraq affecting oil markets.
Global recession leading to a collapse in oil demand.
Political instability across the Middle East and North Africa.
OPEC maintained production causing oil prices to crash.
OPEC and allies agreed to reduce production.
Saudi Arabia and Russia began a price war.
Global lockdowns sharply reduced oil demand.
Oil prices reached historic lows due to oversupply.
War disrupted global energy markets and supply chains.
